skills.label: Frame Inquiry Questions
skills.description: Turn a broad topic into a focused, researchable question with clear scope.

skills.label: Scan Context and Stakeholders
skills.description: Identify relevant contexts, audiences, and stakeholder interests.

skills.label: Map Claims and Evidence
skills.description: Extract main claims and supporting evidence from an argument.

skills.label: Trace Line of Reasoning
skills.description: Explain how an author connects evidence to a conclusion.

skills.label: Evaluate Source Credibility
skills.description: Judge reliability, bias, and usefulness of sources for a purpose.

skills.label: Compare Perspectives
skills.description: Contrast how different lenses frame the same issue.

skills.label: Synthesize Across Sources
skills.description: Integrate multiple sources into a coherent original claim.

skills.label: Address Counterarguments
skills.description: Anticipate and respond to opposing views with warrants.

skills.label: Build Team Argument
skills.description: Coordinate roles and evidence into a shared team claim.

skills.label: Defend Team Work Orally
skills.description: Answer probing questions about process, evidence, and choices.

skills.label: Research from Stimulus Pack
skills.description: Generate and pursue a question grounded in provided stimuli.

skills.label: Craft Individual Written Argument
skills.description: Produce a researched essay with attribution and synthesis.

skills.label: EOCE Part A Analysis
skills.description: Answer short prompts that dissect an argument's reasoning.

skills.label: EOCE Part B Synthesis Essay
skills.description: Write a timed evidence-based argument from a source set.
